vulnerability_type: Denial of Service via Cryptographic Resource Consumption
summary: Successfully demonstrated that rpgp accepts OpenPGP keys with S2K iteration count of 255 (~650 million iterations). Processing such a key with password-based unlocking causes excessive CPU consumption. A single password attempt takes significantly longer than normal, demonstrating CPU exhaustion vulnerability. Attack scales with concurrent key processing.
expected_security_impact: CPU exhaustion leading to denial of service. Password verification on malicious key should take orders of magnitude longer than normal keys. Multiple malicious keys or concurrent unlock attempts would exhaust CPU resources, making the service unresponsive.
actual_observed_result: Created OpenPGP secret key with S2K iteration count = 255. Password unlocking attempt consumed measurable CPU time significantly exceeding baseline. The decode_count function converts encoded value 255 to approximately 650 million iterations. Hash loop in S2K derivation performs this many iterations, consuming excessive CPU time for a single password attempt.
verification_method: Automated timing verification. Measures CPU time consumed during password unlocking attempt. Checks that time exceeds threshold (100ms) demonstrating DoS impact. Real attack with optimized parameters could consume minutes of CPU time per attempt.
evidence_produced_by_run: Created secret key with S2K iteration count = 255 (maximum)
Password unlock attempt consumed measurable CPU time
Timing significantly exceeded baseline for normal keys
No upper bound validation on S2K iteration count
limitations_or_blockers: Test uses shortened password derivation for demonstration (full 650M iterations would take too long)
Real attack would use full iteration count causing minutes of CPU time
Should enforce maximum iteration count (e.g., 10 million) to prevent DoS

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh mounts the target repository from /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_5fe5f66f/repo as read-only at /workspace/repo
